reason: Replace mixed pre-2026 and Nigeria Tax Act logic with one scoped engine effective from 1 January 2026. Small-company status now requires turnover no greater than NGN 50 million, fixed assets no greater than NGN 250 million and no professional-services business. The estimate applies zero CIT and development levy to qualifying small companies; otherwise it applies 30% CIT to total profits and 4% development levy to assessable profits. Section 57 review is flagged rather than calculated for MNE constituents or turnover of at least NGN 20 billion. The pass removes the old NGN 100 million threshold, year selectors that ran the same rules, a medium-company 20% branch, obsolete minimum-tax and tertiary-education-tax labels, and ad hoc profit-base subtraction. Six unit fixtures and seven browser checks are required; no protected golden fixture existed, so there is no carried result delta.
